title: Architecture Review — Cloud Modernisation Platform
scope: Core platform services — API layer, identity, data tier

fitness_for_purpose.summary: The proposed architecture addresses the core modernisation objectives. No significant gaps identified against stated objectives.

findings.assessment: The managed gateway approach satisfies the policy-as-code and multi-region requirements.
Federated identity via the corporate IdP meets the zero-standing-privilege requirement.
The relational data store selection is consistent with the ACID-consistency principle for financial records.

strengths: mTLS between all internal services — consistent with security-requirements.md §3.2
OAuth2/OIDC at the gateway layer — consistent with security-requirements.md §4
Audit logging specified for all authentication events

concerns.description: EU data residency requirement for backup storage is unresolved. The current architecture does not specify whether backup volumes are placed in an EU-compliant region.
Certificate rotation mechanism for mTLS is not yet designed. Manual rotation creates an operational risk.

scalability_concerns: The relational data tier introduces a vertical scaling ceiling; partitioning strategy is not yet defined.
Read-heavy workloads may saturate the primary database replica at projected peak traffic (> 5 000 concurrent users).

compliance_gaps.requirement: EU data residency for backup storage
GDPR data retention policy for audit logs

open_questions: Does EU data residency apply to backup storage volumes?
What is the target RTO/RPO for the DR configuration?
Is a caching layer in scope for v1, or deferred to v2?
recommendations: Resolve EU data residency question before designing the backup architecture.
Design the mTLS certificate rotation mechanism in the next sprint; automate with cert-manager.
Define a database partitioning strategy before the performance test milestone.
Document the penetration test schedule to satisfy security-requirements.md §6.1.
